title: Complete wallet creation: return credentials once, secure listWallets, improve error handling

requirements.text: In the `createWallet` backend function, ensure the return value includes the full wallet credentials — `address`, `privateKey`, `recoveryPhrase`, and `chain` — exactly once upon successful creation. The private key and recovery phrase must only be present in this single response and must never be persisted in stable storage.

quotes: Return the full credentials (address, privateKey, recoveryPhrase, chain) exactly once.

acceptanceCriteria: The `createWallet` function returns a record containing `address`, `privateKey`, `recoveryPhrase`, and `chain` fields on success.
The private key and recovery phrase are not stored in any stable variable or map.
Calling `createWallet` a second time does not expose the credentials of a previously created wallet.

requirements.text: Ensure the `listWallets` backend function returns only wallet addresses (and any non-sensitive metadata such as `chain`). It must never return `privateKey` or `recoveryPhrase` fields.

quotes: Ensure listWallets does not return private keys or seed phrases.

acceptanceCriteria: The `listWallets` return type does not include a `privateKey` field.
The `listWallets` return type does not include a `recoveryPhrase` field.
Each wallet entry returned by `listWallets` contains at minimum the wallet `address` and `chain`.

requirements.text: Update error handling in `createWallet` so that all failure cases return a structured, human-readable error variant instead of propagating raw trap messages. The frontend's `walletErrors.ts` utility should be able to map every returned error variant to a clean user-facing string, eliminating the generic 'Failed to create wallet' message.

quotes: Update error handling so the frontend gets a clean result instead of "Failed to create wallet".

acceptanceCriteria: The `createWallet` backend function returns typed error variants (e.g., `#randomnessError`, `#limitReached`, `#notAuthenticated`, `#internalError`) rather than trapping or returning an untyped string.
The frontend `walletErrors.ts` maps every new error variant to a specific, user-friendly message.
The `EpiqWalletsPanel` component displays the mapped error message and never shows 'Failed to create wallet' to the user.

constraints: Private keys and recovery phrases must never be written to stable storage or any persistent backend variable.
Do not change the wallet address storage schema or the 9-wallet limit already implemented.
Do not modify any file listed under frontend.immutablePaths.
nonGoals: Re-implementing sub-tasks 1–6 (randomness, mnemonic generation, key derivation, address computation, storage, limit enforcement) — those are already complete.
Adding new UI wallet features beyond surfacing the credentials and error messages.
Changing the authentication or session management logic.
